atoms.source_snippet: use alloy_primitives::{Address, Bytes, B256, U256}; ... pub extra_data: Option<Bytes>
atoms.semantic_claim: Ethereum payload attributes carry optional CL-supplied block-header extra_data, with None preserving the EL default extra_data behavior.

destinations.evidence: The destination Alloy Engine API payload attributes own the same optional Ritual extra_data extension.
Beacon payload attributes serialize and deserialize the extra_data extension through Alloy payload attributes.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The source field moved from reth engine-primitives to the Alloy Engine API type used by the v2.2 port, preserving the CL-facing schema field.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Deserialize Engine and Beacon PayloadAttributes with extraData set, assert the field is preserved, assert None omits it without changing the default path, and build a payload whose header uses the requested bytes.
atoms.notes: Reviewed after ba3727354e: Alloy preserves Engine/Beacon payload attribute extra_data serde and conversion, Reth uses the sibling ritual-alloy-internal crates, payload construction consumes the field, and payload_id hashes it.

atoms.semantic_claim: CL-provided payload attributes extra_data is carried into the internal next-block payload-building attributes so block assembly can use dynamic per-block header extra_data.

destinations.evidence: The destination main payload builder resolves CL-supplied PayloadAttributes.extra_data into NextBlockEnvAttributes, falling back to configured default extra_data when the CL field is absent.
The EVM context boundary carries the resolved next-block extra_data into block assembly.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Submit forkchoiceUpdated payload attributes with extraData different from the configured builder default and assert the built block header uses the requested extraData.
atoms.notes: Resolved by ba3727354e; the normal FCU payload-building path now honors CL-supplied extra_data.

atoms.semantic_claim: Payload-building consumers can inspect the optional CL-provided extra_data without taking ownership.

destinations.evidence: The destination exposes the field directly on the public payload attributes type rather than via an accessor.
The downstream consumer surface exposes extra_data directly in the environment attributes used for EVM construction.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination layout uses public fields on split request/context types instead of a method on EthPayloadBuilderAttributes; consumers can still inspect extra_data where the value exists.
atoms.notes: Behavioral risk is captured by the field propagation and payload ID atoms.

atoms.semantic_claim: Payload ID hashing includes length-prefixed extra_data bytes so otherwise identical CL payload requests with different extra_data produce distinct payload IDs.

destinations.snippet: if let Some(extra_data) = &attributes.extra_data { hasher.update((extra_data.len() as u64).to_be_bytes()); hasher.update(extra_data.as_ref()); }
destinations.evidence: The destination payload ID implementation length-prefixes extra_data bytes and has a regression test proving None, Some(empty), and different byte values produce distinct IDs.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Create two PayloadAttributes values identical except extra_data and assert payload_id(parent, attrs_a) differs from payload_id(parent, attrs_b), including a None versus Some(empty) distinction.
atoms.notes: Resolved by ba3727354e; distinct dynamic extra_data requests no longer alias in payload_id.

adversarial_reviews.reason: Cross-repo review confirmed Alloy Engine/Beacon schema preservation, Reth workspace dependency on sibling Alloy crates, payload-builder consumption, and payload ID hashing coverage.

summary: The extra_data schema move to Alloy is reviewed and mapped, while the v2.2 port now exactly carries CL-supplied extra_data through payload construction and payload ID hashing.
notes: Reviewed after ba3727354e: Alloy preserves Engine/Beacon extra_data serde and conversion, Reth uses sibling Alloy crates, payload construction consumes attributes.extra_data, and payload_id length-prefixes extra_data.
